data.description: Crypto Whale Moves $225 Million USDC Before Pulling $77.52 Million Ethereum

data.title: Iran sees BTC as a strategic asset, but USDt prevails for oil tolls: BPI. Bitcoin for payment due to confiscation resistance.
data.description: The Iranian government has opted for Bitcoin as a payment method for oil tolls because of its resilience to confiscation. However, to date, only dollar stablecoins have been utilized for this purpose.

data.title: How Will Dow Jones Futures, Oil Prices React As Iran, Hormuz News Turns?
data.description: As Iran asserts its continued strict control over the Strait of Hormuz, market participants are closely watching for potential reactions. The status of this vital waterway could impact global markets, particularly with Tesla's earnings on the horizon. Traders are monitoring Dow Jones futures and oil prices for any signs of volatility or shifts in response to the ongoing developments between Iran and the strategic water passage. The situation remains fluid, and investors are on high alert for any potential market implications.

data.title: Hollywood talent agency, previously Wasserman, now The Team, receives interest from buyers after founder's ties to Epstein revealed.
data.description: The agency previously known as Wasserman has rebranded to The Team and is now up for sale following the exposure of its founder's emails in the Epstein files.

data.title: Pastor Dino Rizzo Marks 100 Days to Serve Day Ahead of Global Outreach Initiative
data.description: Pastor Dino Rizzo recently emphasized the significance of reaching the 100-day mark leading up to Serve Day 2026. Thousands of churches are gearing up to engage in acts of compassion on July 11th to benefit their communities. This event brings together church leaders and teams in preparation for the impactful day. The announcement was made in Birmingham, Alabama on April 18, 2026.

data.title: Inspira Completes Commercial Deployment of its AME System at a Tier-1 U.S. Defense Entity
data.description: Inspira™ Technologies OXY B.H.N. Ltd., an Israel-based company, recently disclosed that they have effectively delivered and had an Additively Manufactured Electronics ("AME") system accepted at a Tier-1 U.S. defense customer site. This accomplishment was done under a commercial agreement and marks a significant milestone for the company. The company's stock is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol IINN.

data.title: ENvue Issues Stockholder Letter & Provides Corporate Update
data.description: Today, on April 16, 2026, in Tyler, Texas, ENvue Medical, Inc. (NASDAQ: FEED), a company focused on medical technology, particularly in non-invasive solutions for enteral care in clinical and home care environments, provided a stockholder letter and corporate update.

data.title: Pliant Therapeutics Announces Presentation of Updated Data from the Phase 1 Trial of PLN-101095  in Patients with ICI-Refractory Solid Tumors at the 2026 AACR Annual Meeting
data.description: The study results revealed that the responses were confirmed with an average maximum tumor reduction of 89% from the initial baseline. Additionally, the median time on treatment extended to 19 months.

data.title: Vipboss Marks Earth Day with Renewed Commitment to Green Energy Solutions
data.description: On April 18, 2026, in New York, Vipboss, a leading manufacturer and developer of lithium iron phosphate (LiFePO4) battery packs for energy storage and mobility uses, highlighted its commitment to environmental responsibility as Earth Day approaches. Vipboss focuses on producing high-quality LiFePO4 battery packs for a range of applications, emphasizing its dedication to sustainability and innovation.

data.title: ‘Hollow’: Trump’s elusive endgame
data.description: In a televised address on Wednesday night, President Donald Trump stated that the U.S. is close to putting an end to the threat posed by Iran.

data.title: Assessing the damage
data.description: Amidst escalating oil and gas prices, increasing expenses for essential commodities such as aluminum and helium, as well as trillions of dollars in losses from investment portfolios, American households are managing comparatively better amidst the conflict with Iran.

data.title: Kitchen countertop politics
data.description: For nearly ten years, Cambria CEO Marty Davis has been working diligently to prevent the influx of foreign quartz countertops into the U.S. market. There is a chance that President Donald Trump will have the opportunity to address this issue.

data.title: 5 takeaways from POLITICO’s Economy Summit
data.description: Key figures from the White House, Capitol Hill, and Wall Street convened on Wednesday for a discussion hosted by POLITICO. The event delved into the impacts of the conflict in Iran, the advancements in artificial intelligence, and a recent Supreme Court ruling on the American public and overall economy. This forum aimed to analyze these pressing issues that are currently influencing and shaping various aspects of society.

data.title: The terrible twos
data.description: Auctions for government debt are usually uneventful; however, when they become more exciting, it may signify underlying issues.

data.title: The ‘endgame’ is here
data.description: Last week, American banks achieved a significant victory as regulators revealed a substantial revision to bank capital requirements, reducing the necessary amount of capital that banks must retain by a considerable margin.

data.title: Higher for longer for oil and rates
data.description: Currently, there is a 30% probability on Wall Street that interest rates will increase following the last meeting of the Federal Reserve in 2026.

data.title: The Fed’s flying blind
data.description: Central banks around the globe are expected to adopt a more cautious stance on inflation in a series of meetings scheduled for this week.

data.title: There’s always a housing angle
data.description: Worries regarding potential inflation down the line have led to an increase in yields on government bonds, impacting the rates of home loans.

data.title: Q-Day poses threat to Bitcoin with quantum computers capable of forging digital signatures, risking unauthorized transactions.
data.description: Quantum computers pose a potential threat to Bitcoin security as they could potentially manipulate digital signatures, enabling unauthorized transactions to be made. This issue has raised concerns among experts in the field, highlighting the importance of safeguarding the cryptographic protocols that underpin the security of Bitcoin transactions. This potential vulnerability underscores the need for ongoing research and development in the field of quantum-resistant cryptography to protect the integrity of digital currencies. A possible consequence of this threat is the compromise of Bitcoin's security measures, potentially leading to financial losses and eroding trust in the cryptocurrency system. It is imperative to address these concerns proactively to mitigate the risks associated with quantum computing advancements in the future.
